family_summary: A Soviet intervention that began with the December 1979 entry and regime-change operation in Kabul, evolved into a long, spatially fragmented counterinsurgency fought alongside the Democratic Republic of Afghanistan (DRA), and ended with staged Soviet withdrawal in February 1989. Because violence, belligerent structure, and exposed populations varied sharply across urban seizure, valley offensives, border-corridor relief operations, and withdrawal-period bombing, episode splitting is necessary.
notes: Episode-first research bundle. Full quantitative estimate completed only for the Panjshir 1982 campaign; other episodes are proposed and source-screened but not fully quantified.
